preamble: The transformation from a voiced alveolar nasal [n] to a voiced retroflex flap [ɽ]. This complex shift involves both retroflexion and denasalization, typically found in Indo-Aryan and some Scandinavian dialects.

examples.note: Intervocalic nasals in some dialects weaken to retroflex flaps via an intermediate [ɳ] stage.
In some 'thick L' dialects, the /rn/ cluster can be realized as a retroflex flap rather than a nasal.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
